# CWE-257 negative: one-way hash instead of reversible encryption (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-257-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
)

func digestSecret(plain string) (string, error) {
	salt := make([]byte, 16)
	if _, err := rand.Read(salt); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	sum := sha256.Sum256(append(salt, []byte(plain)...))
	return hex.EncodeToString(salt) + ":" + hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]), nil
}

// CWE-257 Storing Passwords in a Recoverable Format (mitigated)
func PersistLoginSecret(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB) {
	plain := r.FormValue("pass")
	digest, err := digestSecret(plain)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "hash", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	login := r.FormValue("login")
	_, err = db.Exec("INSERT INTO logins(login, secret) VALUES(?, ?)", login, digest)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "save", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"status": "stored"})
}
